summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 9 warning, 9 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: рџџЎ `.team/features/git-worktree-isolation` вЂ” No SPEC.md exists. Feature #17 (document-driven development) requires an approved spec before coding. This feature was implemented directly from the roadmap line item. Add to backlog: enforce `agt brainstorm` в†’ SPEC.md approval even for roadmap-sourced features.
рџџЎ `bin/lib/gate.mjs:21` вЂ” `|| process.cwd()` fallback not caught by the grep-audit test (regex matches `cwd: process.cwd()` but not `|| process.cwd()`); add a second assertion to the audit suite

рџџЎ `bin/lib/run.mjs:1185` вЂ” Builder handshake written by agent into `worktreePath/.team/...` is never found by harness validator reading from `mainCwd/.team/...`; silently skipped via `existsSync` returning false; agent brief should specify an absolute path or shared artifact location; add integration test covering this path

рџџЎ `test/worktree.test.mjs:539` вЂ” "Worktree preserved on error" tests are regex source assertions; a valid refactor of the catch block could break behavior without failing them; replace with an integration test that injects an actual throw

рџџЎ `bin/lib/run.mjs:345` вЂ” `dispatchToAgentAsync` returns `ok:false` for any non-claude agent; `runParallelReviews` calls this for all roles; codex users get all parallel reviews silently failing; add test + warning log

рџџЎ `bin/lib/gate.mjs:21` вЂ” `cmdGate` falls back to `process.cwd()` when `--cwd` is not passed; an agent invoking `agt-harness gate` directly without `--cwd` silently bypasses worktree isolation. The required-cwd contract covers `runGateInline`/`dispatchToAgent`/`dispatchToAgentAsync` but not `cmdGate`. Add to backlog.

рџџЎ `test/worktree.test.mjs:549` вЂ” Source-assertion greps for literal log string `"preserving worktree"` in the catch block. Breaks on any log reword without behavior change. The behavioral invariant (worktree reusable after error) is already covered at line 558; remove this regex test.

рџџЎ `test/worktree.test.mjs:657` вЂ” Two brace-tracking source-scanners (~80 lines) verify no `cwd: process.cwd()` inside `dispatchToAgent`/`dispatchToAgentAsync`. This invariant is already covered behaviorally at lines 322вЂ“364 (mock-spawnSync asserts `opts.cwd === worktreePath`). The source scans are redundant, fragile to reformatting, and more complex than the behavioral tests they shadow.
No dead code, premature abstractions, unnecessary indirection, or gold-plating found in production code. The two рџџЎ items are test-only complexity and go to backlog.
